description: n8n-orchestrated workflow that watches Google Calendar for upcoming appointments, extracts structured data via LLM, and triggers a Retell AI voice agent ('Anne') to call the patient and confirm the appointment.

trigger.description: Fires when an appointment event is starting within the configured lookahead window

steps.name: Google Calendar Trigger
steps.description: Detects an appointment event starting within the next 12 hours

steps.name: Extract Structured Appointment Data
steps.description: Uses an LLM to convert the raw calendar event into structured JSON fields
steps.system_message: You are an assistant. Generate structured JSON with the following fields: name (full name of the recipient), phone_number (string), reason (reason for the appointment), start_time (ISO 8601 datetime), end_time (ISO 8601 datetime), email. Output ONLY in this exact JSON format. No comments, no extra text.

steps.name: Trigger Outbound Call via Retell AI
steps.description: Calls the Retell AI API to place an outbound phone call, passing appointment details as dynamic variables for the agent to use

steps.name: Agent Identifies Recipient
steps.description: Agent asks whether it's speaking with the intended recipient

branches.note: Continue script, prefaced with a message-relay intro

steps.name: Introduction
steps.description: Agent introduces herself and states the purpose of the call
steps.script: This is Anne calling from Smile Dental Clinic. I'm reaching out to remind you about your upcoming dental appointment.

steps.name: Provide Appointment Details
steps.description: Agent states the reason and time window for the appointment, converting ISO timestamps to natural spoken language
steps.script: Your appointment for {{reason}} is scheduled between {{start_time}} and {{end_time}}.

steps.name: Confirm Availability
steps.description: Agent asks if the scheduled time still works for the patient
steps.script: Does that time still work for you?

steps.name: Clarify Response
steps.description: Agent restates appointment details to clarify an unclear response
steps.script: Just to confirm — your appointment is between {{start_time}} and {{end_time}} for {{reason}}. Would you like to keep that time?

steps.name: Transfer Call to Human Staff
steps.description: Transfers the call to a human clinic representative to handle rescheduling or cancellation

steps.name: End Call - Appointment Confirmed
steps.description: Agent thanks the patient and politely ends the call after confirming the appointment
steps.script: Great! We look forward to seeing you then. Is there anything else I can help you with today?

steps.name: End Call - Recipient Unavailable
steps.description: Agent ends the call when the recipient is unavailable and no message could be left
steps.script: No problem, I'll try reaching out later. Thank you and have a great day!

transfer_call.description: Transfers the active call to a human representative when the patient wants to reschedule or cancel

end_call.description: Ends the call politely, either because the conversation is complete or the recipient is unavailable

conversation_notes: Always convert ISO 8601 timestamps into natural spoken language before speaking (e.g. '2025-04-12T15:30:00' becomes 'Three thirty PM on Saturday, April 12th').
Never read raw timestamps, seconds, or timezone codes aloud.
Keep responses concise, engaging, proactive, and non-repetitive.

error_handling.call_not_answered: Log appointment_status as 'no_answer' and optionally retry or fall back to SMS
error_handling.retell_api_failure: Retry the HTTP request up to 3 times, then flag for manual staff follow-up
error_handling.llm_extraction_failure: Flag the calendar event for manual review if required fields cannot be extracted

data_privacy_notes: Patient name, phone number, email, and appointment reason are sensitive data — encrypt in transit and at rest
Store Retell AI and n8n API keys as environment variables/secrets, never hard-coded or committed to source control
Comply with applicable healthcare data privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, or local equivalents)
